Comfort meets performance. Based on our high-tech high-comfort BFC line of adult boots the Reverb is a unisex boot that offers a comfy accommodating fit and a supportive flex for your growing ski star.

Size22.5, 23.5, 24.5, 25.5, 26.5, 27.5, 28.5GenderYouthAbilityIntermediateCategoryAll Mountain, 2 PieceCollectionBFCSki Boot Cuff MaterialPPSki Boot Flex Rating70Ski Boot LinerCushfit ComfortSki Boot OutsoleGripWalkSki Boot ShellPPSki Boot Strap30mm VelcroSki Boot VolumeHighWidth103mm

TechnologyPP Shell:Polypropylene is a lightweight, forgiving plastic used in ski boot shells to provide easy flexing and accessible comfort. It’s ideal for entry-level or junior skiers who are still learning proper technique.PP Cuff:Lightweight and durable, our polypropylene cuff delivers consistent flex and reliable support in all conditions. Engineered for comfort and performance, it’s an ideal choice for skiers seeking value without compromising on control.Cushfit Comfort:Same great fit and out-of-the-box feel as our classic Cushfit liner, but with additional foam in key areas for increased comfort and compliance30MM Velcro Strap:Slim and simple, the 30mm VELCRO® strap is lightweight and user-friendly, offering a secure closure for recreational skiing. Perfect for boots where comfort and convenience are top priorities.GripWalk:The sole-binding system for better walking comfort and top skiing performance. GripWalk contains of a new co-polymer sole with rocker profile. The convex shape and ribbed tread of the rubber add a lot of walking comfort and provide a much better grip. Still, integrated pads made of stiff and rigid materials, enable great power transmission and more precise release function of the binding. GripWalk soles are the perfect tuning parts designed for selected premium ski boots and bindings.Fully Heat-Moldable:Both the boot's shell and cuff are capable of being heat-molded to a variety of foot shapes for a true custom fit.Hands Free Entry:Dual material overlap for intuitive and easy hands-free exit and entry.TRUST A PROFESSIONAL:Before using your new boots, you must have an authorized K2 Ski retailer check your boot, binding, and sole setup. K2 Ski boot soles must only be changed by an authorized retailer.
